parameters.command: =GW=/app/cubeos/claude-context && LOCK_FILE="{{ $('Derive Slot').first().json.lockFile }}" && if [ -f $GW/$LOCK_FILE ]; then if [ $(( $(date +%s) - $(stat -c %Y $GW/$LOCK_FILE) )) -gt 600 ]; then echo FREE; else echo LOCKED; fi; else echo FREE; fi

=GW="/app/cubeos/claude-context"
DB="$GW/gateway.db"
MSG_B64="{{ $('Detect Command').first().json.base64Message }}"
ROOM="{{ $('Extract Messages').first().json.sourceRoom }}"
LOCK_FILE="{{ $('Derive Slot').first().json.lockFile }}"

# Get issue ID from slot-specific lock file
ISSUE_ID=""
if [ -f "$GW/$LOCK_FILE" ]; then
  ISSUE_ID=$(cat "$GW/$LOCK_FILE" | tr -d '\n')
fi

PIDFILE="/tmp/claude-pid-${ISSUE_ID}"
INJECT="/tmp/claude-inject-${ISSUE_ID}.json"

# Try inject if PID file exists and PID is alive
if [ -n "$ISSUE_ID" ] && [ -f "$PIDFILE" ]; then
  PID=$(cat "$PIDFILE" | tr -d '\n')
  if kill -0 $PID 2>/dev/null; then
    MSG_TEXT=$(echo "$MSG_B64" | base64 -d)
    python3 -c "import json,sys; json.dump({'message': sys.stdin.read()}, open('$INJECT','w'))" <<< "$MSG_TEXT"
    kill -INT $PID 2>/dev/null
    echo "INJECTED:$ISSUE_ID"
    exit 0
  fi
fi

# Fallback: queue the message (room-aware)
ROOM_PREFIX=""
case "$ROOM" in
  *miZJJDwFQZDkuMcBqL*) ROOM_PREFIX="MESHSAT-" ;;
  *iXTnQsFJahUquYPDdG*) ROOM_PREFIX="CUBEOS-" ;;
  *AOMuEtXGyzGFLgObKN*) ROOM_PREFIX="IFRNLLEI01PRD-" ;;
  *NKosBPujbWMevzHaaM*) ROOM_PREFIX="IFRGRSKG01PRD-" ;;
esac
if [ -n "$ROOM_PREFIX" ]; then
  sqlite3 "$DB" "INSERT INTO queue (issue_id, message) SELECT COALESCE(issue_id,''), '$MSG_B64' FROM sessions WHERE issue_id LIKE '${ROOM_PREFIX}%' ORDER BY last_active DESC LIMIT 1;"
else
  sqlite3 "$DB" "INSERT INTO queue (issue_id, message) SELECT COALESCE(issue_id,''), '$MSG_B64' FROM sessions WHERE is_current=1;"
fi
echo "QUEUED"

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ msgtype: 'm.notice', body: 'Claude is busy processing. Your message has been queued and will be delivered when the current task completes.' }) }}

parameters.command: ={{ "touch /app/cubeos/claude-context/" + $('Derive Slot').first().json.lockFile }}

=PREFIX={{ $('Detect Command').first().json.prefix }}
ROOM="{{ $('Extract Messages').first().json.sourceRoom }}"

sqlite3 $DB "ALTER TABLE sessions ADD COLUMN last_response_b64 TEXT DEFAULT '';" 2>/dev/null

if [ -n "$PREFIX" ]; then
  RESULT=$(sqlite3 $DB "SELECT json_object('sessionId', COALESCE(session_id,''), 'issueId', COALESCE(issue_id,''), 'issue_title', COALESCE(issue_title,''), 'paused', COALESCE(paused,0), 'lastResponseB64', COALESCE(last_response_b64,'')) FROM sessions WHERE issue_id='$PREFIX' LIMIT 1;")
else
  ROOM_PREFIX=""
  case "$ROOM" in
    *miZJJDwFQZDkuMcBqL*) ROOM_PREFIX="MESHSAT-" ;;
    *iXTnQsFJahUquYPDdG*) ROOM_PREFIX="CUBEOS-" ;;
    *AOMuEtXGyzGFLgObKN*) ROOM_PREFIX="IFRNLLEI01PRD-" ;;
    *NKosBPujbWMevzHaaM*) ROOM_PREFIX="IFRGRSKG01PRD-" ;;
  esac
  if [ -n "$ROOM_PREFIX" ]; then
    RESULT=$(sqlite3 $DB "SELECT json_object('sessionId', COALESCE(session_id,''), 'issueId', COALESCE(issue_id,''), 'issue_title', COALESCE(issue_title,''), 'paused', COALESCE(paused,0), 'lastResponseB64', COALESCE(last_response_b64,'')) FROM sessions WHERE issue_id LIKE '${ROOM_PREFIX}%' ORDER BY last_active DESC LIMIT 1;")
  else
    RESULT=$(sqlite3 $DB "SELECT json_object('sessionId', COALESCE(session_id,''), 'issueId', COALESCE(issue_id,''), 'issue_title', COALESCE(issue_title,''), 'paused', COALESCE(paused,0), 'lastResponseB64', COALESCE(last_response_b64,'')) FROM sessions WHERE is_current=1 LIMIT 1;")
  fi

[ -n "$RESULT" ] && echo "$RESULT" || echo '{"sessionId":"","issueId":"","issue_title":"","paused":0,"lastResponseB64":""}'

=PREFIX={{ $('Detect Command').first().json.prefix }}
B64MSG={{ $('Detect Command').first().json.base64Message }}
ROOM="{{ $('Extract Messages').first().json.sourceRoom }}"
DB=/app/cubeos/claude-context/gateway.db
if [ -n "$PREFIX" ]; then
  FILTER="issue_id='$PREFIX'"
else
  ROOM_PREFIX=""
  case "$ROOM" in
    *miZJJDwFQZDkuMcBqL*) ROOM_PREFIX="MESHSAT-" ;;
    *iXTnQsFJahUquYPDdG*) ROOM_PREFIX="CUBEOS-" ;;
    *AOMuEtXGyzGFLgObKN*) ROOM_PREFIX="IFRNLLEI01PRD-" ;;
  esac
  if [ -n "$ROOM_PREFIX" ]; then
    FILTER="issue_id LIKE '${ROOM_PREFIX}%'"
  else
    FILTER="is_current=1"
  fi
fi
PAUSED=$(sqlite3 $DB "SELECT COALESCE(paused,0) FROM sessions WHERE $FILTER ORDER BY last_active DESC LIMIT 1;" 2>/dev/null)
if [ "$PAUSED" = "1" ]; then
  ISSUE=$(sqlite3 $DB "SELECT issue_id FROM sessions WHERE $FILTER ORDER BY last_active DESC LIMIT 1;")
  sqlite3 $DB "INSERT INTO queue (issue_id, message) VALUES ('$ISSUE', '$B64MSG');"
  echo PAUSED
else
  ISSUE=$(sqlite3 $DB "SELECT issue_id FROM sessions WHERE $FILTER ORDER BY last_active DESC LIMIT 1;")
  sqlite3 $DB "SELECT message FROM queue WHERE issue_id='$ISSUE' ORDER BY id ASC;"
  sqlite3 $DB "DELETE FROM queue WHERE issue_id='$ISSUE';"
fi

parameters.jsCode:
const sub = $json.sub || '';

let body = '';

if (!sub) {
  body = 'Available commands:\n'
    + '!session    \u2014 Manage active Claude sessions\n'
    + '!issue      \u2014 Manage YouTrack issues\n'
    + '!pipeline   \u2014 Monitor GitLab CI pipelines\n'
    + '!system     \u2014 View system status\n'
    + '!gateway    \u2014 Switch between online/offline mode\n'
    + '!mode       \u2014 Switch gateway routing mode\n'
    + '!debug      \u2014 Dump raw gateway state\n'
    + '!learn      \u2014 Teacher agent (in #learning): lessons + quizzes + progress\n'
    + '!help <command> \u2014 Show subcommands for a command\n'
    + '\n'
    + 'Legacy shortcuts: !done, !status, !cancel\n'
    + 'Mode shortcuts: !offline, !online\n'
    + 'Teacher shortcuts: !quiz, !progress, !digest, !leaderboard, !skip, !teach, !grade';
} else if (sub === 'session') {
  body = '!session commands:\n'
    + 'current              \u2014 Show current active session details\n'
    + 'list                 \u2014 List all active sessions\n'
    + 'done [issue-id]      \u2014 End session \u2192 YouTrack To Verify\n'
    + 'cancel [issue-id]    \u2014 Hard abort session, clear lock + queue\n'
    + 'pause [issue-id]     \u2014 Hold queue, stop processing messages\n'
    + 'resume [issue-id]    \u2014 Resume paused session\n'
    + 'switch <issue-id>    \u2014 Change current session\n'
    + 'log [issue-id]       \u2014 Last 50 lines of execution log\n'
    + '\n'
    + '[issue-id] is optional \u2014 defaults to current session if omitted.';
} else if (sub === 'issue') {
  body = '!issue commands:\n'
    + '\n'
    + 'State transitions:\n'
    + 'open <id>           \u2014 Move to Open (ends active session)\n'
    + 'inprogress <id>     \u2014 Move to In Progress (starts session)\n'
    + 'toverify <id>       \u2014 Move to To Verify (ends session if active)\n'
    + 'done <id>           \u2014 Move to Done (ends session if active)\n'
    + '\n'
    + 'Aliases:\n'
    + 'start <id>          \u2014 Alias for inprogress\n'
    + 'verify <id>         \u2014 Alias for toverify\n'
    + 'close <id>          \u2014 Alias for done\n'
    + 'stop <id>           \u2014 Move back to Open (no session change)\n'
    + '\n'
    + 'Other:\n'
    + 'status              \u2014 List In Progress + Open issues\n'
    + 'info <id>           \u2014 Show issue details\n'
    + 'comment <id> <text> \u2014 Post comment to issue\n'
    + '\n'
    + 'Example: !issue inprogress CUBEOS-21';
} else if (sub === 'pipeline') {
  body = '!pipeline commands:\n'
    + 'status           \u2014 All repos latest pipeline status\n'
    + 'status <repo>    \u2014 Last 5 pipelines for a specific repo\n'
    + 'logs <repo>      \u2014 Logs from latest failed pipeline\n'
    + 'retry <repo>     \u2014 Retry latest failed pipeline\n'
    + 'Repos: api, dashboard, hal, coreapps, releases, meshsat';
} else if (sub === 'system') {
  body = '!system commands:\n'
    + 'status     \u2014 System snapshot (load, memory, disk, uptime)\n'
    + 'processes  \u2014 Claude and n8n process list';
} else if (sub === 'gateway') {
  body = '!gateway commands:\n'
    + 'offline  \u2014 Switch to local Ollama model (devstral-small-2)\n'
    + 'online   \u2014 Switch to Anthropic cloud API\n'
    + 'status   \u2014 Show current mode and session\n'
    + '\n'
    + 'Shortcuts: !offline, !online\n'
    + 'Active session blocks mode switching.';
} else if (sub === 'mode') {
  body = '!mode commands:\n'
    + 'status     \u2014 Show current routing mode\n'
    + 'oc-cc      \u2014 OpenClaw \u2192 Claude Code (default)\n'
    + 'oc-oc      \u2014 OpenClaw \u2192 OpenClaw (fully local)\n'
    + 'cc-cc      \u2014 Claude Code only (legacy)\n'
    + 'cc-oc      \u2014 n8n session \u2192 OpenClaw backend\n'
    + '\n'
    + 'Example: !mode oc-cc';
} else if (sub === 'debug') {
  body = '!debug\n'
    + 'Dumps raw gateway state: lock file, sessions DB, queue count,\n'
    + 'cooldown files, mode (online/offline), and claude processes.\n'
    + 'No subcommands \u2014 just type !debug.';
} else if (sub === 'learn' || sub === 'teacher' || sub === 'quiz' || sub === 'progress' || sub === 'digest' || sub === 'leaderboard' || sub === 'skip' || sub === 'teach' || sub === 'grade') {
  // IFRNLLEI01PRD-654 teacher agent — operator-facing help.
  body = '!learn commands (teacher agent; classroom = #learning):\n'
    + '\n'
    + '!learn                      \u2014 Pick up where you left off (SM-2 due topic or first-run bootstrap)\n'
    + '!learn <topic-id>           \u2014 Lesson on a specific topic (see !progress for ids)\n'
    + '!learn pause | resume       \u2014 Pause / resume scheduling for your account\n'
    + '!learn public on | off      \u2014 Opt in / out of leaderboard and !progress public\n'
    + '\n'
    + '!quiz                       \u2014 Quiz on the current due topic\n'
    + '!quiz <topic-id>            \u2014 Quiz on a specific topic\n'
    + '!grade <session-id> <text> \u2014 Submit your answer for grading (session id is in the quiz DM)\n'
    + '!progress                   \u2014 Your mastery distribution (DM)\n'
    + '!progress public            \u2014 Post to #learning (requires !learn public on)\n'
    + '!digest | !leaderboard      \u2014 Aggregate class digest in #learning\n'
    + '!skip | !teach              \u2014 Skip the current topic or request a new lesson\n'
    + '\n'
    + 'Authorisation: must be a joined member of #learning.\n'
    + 'Runbook: docs/runbooks/teacher-agent.md';
} else {
  body = '!' + sub + ' \u2014 unknown command.\n\n' +
    'Available commands:\n' +
    '!session    \u2014 Manage active Claude sessions\n' +
    '!issue      \u2014 Manage YouTrack issues\n' +
    '!pipeline   \u2014 Monitor GitLab CI pipelines\n' +
    '!system     \u2014 View system status\n' +
    '!gateway    \u2014 Switch between online/offline mode\n' +
    '!mode       \u2014 Switch gateway routing mode\n' +
    '!debug      \u2014 Dump raw gateway state\n' +
    '!learn      \u2014 Teacher agent (in #learning): lessons + quizzes + progress\n' +
    '!help <cmd> \u2014 Show subcommands for a command\n' +
    '\n' +
    'Legacy shortcuts: !done, !status, !cancel\n' +
    'Mode shortcuts: !offline, !online\n' +
    'Teacher shortcuts: !quiz, !progress, !digest, !leaderboard, !skip, !teach, !grade';
}

const matrixBody = JSON.stringify({ msgtype: 'm.notice', body });

return [{ json: { matrixBody, isDone: false, triggerData: null } }];

parameters.command:
=SUB="{{ $('Detect Command').first().json.sub }}"
RAW_ARG1="{{ $('Detect Command').first().json.args[0] || '' }}"
RAW_ARGREST="{{ $('Detect Command').first().json.args.slice(1).join(' ') }}"
ROOM="{{ $('Extract Messages').first().json.sourceRoom }}"
YT_URL="https://youtrack.example.net"
YT_TOKEN="REDACTED_YT_TOKEN"
DB=/app/cubeos/claude-context/gateway.db
GW=/app/cubeos/claude-context

ARG1=$(echo "$RAW_ARG1" | tr '[:lower:]' '[:upper:]')
ARGREST="$RAW_ARGREST"

# Room-aware project mapping
YT_PROJECT="CUBEOS"
case "$ROOM" in
  *miZJJDwFQZDkuMcBqL*) YT_PROJECT="MESHSAT" ;;
  *iXTnQsFJahUquYPDdG*) YT_PROJECT="CUBEOS" ;;
  *AOMuEtXGyzGFLgObKN*) YT_PROJECT="IFRNLLEI01PRD" ;;
esac

ytcurl() {
  curl -s --max-time 10 -H "Authorization: Bearer $YT_TOKEN" "$@"
}

lookupNumericId() {
  ytcurl "$YT_URL/api/issues/$1?fields=id" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('id',''))" 2>/dev/null
}

patchState() {
  local NUMID="$1"
  local STATE="$2"
  curl -s --max-time 10 -w "\nHTTP_STATUS:%{http_code}" \
    -X POST \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer $YT_TOKEN" \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -d "{\"customFields\":[{\"\$type\":\"StateIssueCustomField\",\"name\":\"State\",\"value\":{\"name\":\"$STATE\"}}]}" \
    "$YT_URL/api/issues/$NUMID"
}

case "$SUB" in
  status)
    ytcurl -w "\nHTTP_STATUS:%{http_code}" "$YT_URL/api/issues?query=project%3A${YT_PROJECT}%20State%3A%7BIn%20Progress%7D%2COpen&fields=idReadable,summary,customFields(name,value(name))&\$top=20" 2>&1
    ;;
  info)
    [ -z "$ARG1" ] && echo "INVALID_ID:none" && exit 0
    echo "$ARG1" | grep -qE '^[A-Z0-9]+-[0-9]+$' || { echo "INVALID_ID:$ARG1"; exit 0; }
    ytcurl -w "\nHTTP_STATUS:%{http_code}" "$YT_URL/api/issues/$ARG1?fields=idReadable,summary,description,created,updated,tags(name),customFields(name,value(name,login))" 2>&1
    ;;
  start|inprogress)
    [ -z "$ARG1" ] && echo "INVALID_ID:none" && exit 0
    echo "$ARG1" | grep -qE '^[A-Z0-9]+-[0-9]+$' || { echo "INVALID_ID:$ARG1"; exit 0; }
    NUMID=$(lookupNumericId "$ARG1")
    [ -z "$NUMID" ] && echo "LOOKUP_FAILED:$ARG1" && exit 0
    LOCK_FILE="$GW/gateway.lock"
    if [ -f "$LOCK_FILE" ]; then
      LOCK_AGE=$(( $(date +%s) - $(stat -c %Y "$LOCK_FILE") ))
      if [ "$LOCK_AGE" -lt 600 ]; then
        LOCK_ISSUE=$(cat "$LOCK_FILE" 2>/dev/null)
        echo "ACTIVE_SESSION:$LOCK_ISSUE"
        exit 0
      fi
    fi
    echo "$ARG1" > "$LOCK_FILE"
    patchState "$NUMID" "In Progress" >/dev/null 2>&1
    ISSUE_JSON=$(ytcurl "$YT_URL/api/issues/$ARG1?fields=idReadable,summary,description,comments(text,author(login),created)")
    SUMMARY=$(echo "$ISSUE_JSON" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('summary',''))" 2>/dev/null)
    DESC=$(echo "$ISSUE_JSON" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('description',''))" 2>/dev/null)
    COMMENTS=$(echo "$ISSUE_JSON" | python3 -c "
import sys,json
d=json.load(sys.stdin)
for c in d.get('comments',[]):
    a=c.get('author',{}).get('login','?')
    t=c.get('text','')
    print(a+': '+t)
" 2>/dev/null)
    B64_SUMMARY=$(echo "$SUMMARY" | base64 -w0)
    B64_DESC=$(echo "$DESC" | base64 -w0)
    B64_COMMENTS=$(echo "$COMMENTS" | base64 -w0)
    echo "START_SESSION:$ARG1"
    echo "B64_SUMMARY:$B64_SUMMARY"
    echo "B64_DESC:$B64_DESC"
    echo "B64_COMMENTS:$B64_COMMENTS"
    ;;
  open)
    [ -z "$ARG1" ] && echo "INVALID_ID:none" && exit 0
    echo "$ARG1" | grep -qE '^[A-Z0-9]+-[0-9]+$' || { echo "INVALID_ID:$ARG1"; exit 0; }
    NUMID=$(lookupNumericId "$ARG1")
    [ -z "$NUMID" ] && echo "LOOKUP_FAILED:$ARG1" && exit 0
    patchState "$NUMID" "Open"
    touch "$GW/gateway.cooldown.$ARG1"
    SESSION_JSON=$(sqlite3 "$DB" "SELECT json_object('sessionId', session_id, 'issueId', issue_id) FROM sessions WHERE issue_id='$ARG1';" 2>/dev/null)
    if [ -n "$SESSION_JSON" ]; then
      rm -f "$GW/gateway.lock"
      sqlite3 "$DB" "DELETE FROM queue WHERE issue_id='$ARG1';" 2>/dev/null
      sqlite3 "$DB" "INSERT INTO session_log (issue_id, issue_title, session_id, started_at, ended_at, message_count, outcome, schema_version) SELECT issue_id, issue_title, session_id, started_at, datetime('now'), message_count, 'open', 1 FROM sessions WHERE issue_id='$ARG1';" 2>/dev/null
      sqlite3 "$DB" "DELETE FROM sessions WHERE issue_id='$ARG1';" 2>/dev/null
      echo ""
      echo "SESSION_CLEANED:$ARG1"
    fi
    ;;
  stop)
    [ -z "$ARG1" ] && echo "INVALID_ID:none" && exit 0
    echo "$ARG1" | grep -qE '^[A-Z0-9]+-[0-9]+$' || { echo "INVALID_ID:$ARG1"; exit 0; }
    NUMID=$(lookupNumericId "$ARG1")
    [ -z "$NUMID" ] && echo "LOOKUP_FAILED:$ARG1" && exit 0
    patchState "$NUMID" "Open"
    touch "$GW/gateway.cooldown.$ARG1"
    ;;
  verify|toverify)
    [ -z "$ARG1" ] && echo "INVALID_ID:none" && exit 0
    echo "$ARG1" | grep -qE '^[A-Z0-9]+-[0-9]+$' || { echo "INVALID_ID:$ARG1"; exit 0; }
    NUMID=$(lookupNumericId "$ARG1")
    [ -z "$NUMID" ] && echo "LOOKUP_FAILED:$ARG1" && exit 0
    patchState "$NUMID" "To Verify"
    touch "$GW/gateway.cooldown.$ARG1"
    SESSION_JSON=$(sqlite3 "$DB" "SELECT json_object('sessionId', session_id, 'issueId', issue_id) FROM sessions WHERE issue_id='$ARG1' AND is_current=1;" 2>/dev/null)
    if [ -n "$SESSION_JSON" ]; then
      echo ""
      echo "HAS_SESSION:$SESSION_JSON"
    fi
    ;;
  done|close)
    [ -z "$ARG1" ] && echo "INVALID_ID:none" && exit 0
    echo "$ARG1" | grep -qE '^[A-Z0-9]+-[0-9]+$' || { echo "INVALID_ID:$ARG1"; exit 0; }
    NUMID=$(lookupNumericId "$ARG1")
    [ -z "$NUMID" ] && echo "LOOKUP_FAILED:$ARG1" && exit 0
    patchState "$NUMID" "Done"
    touch "$GW/gateway.cooldown.$ARG1"
    SESSION_JSON=$(sqlite3 "$DB" "SELECT json_object('sessionId', session_id, 'issueId', issue_id) FROM sessions WHERE issue_id='$ARG1' AND is_current=1;" 2>/dev/null)
    if [ -n "$SESSION_JSON" ]; then
      echo ""
      echo "HAS_SESSION:$SESSION_JSON"
    fi
    ;;
  comment)
    [ -z "$ARG1" ] && echo "INVALID_ID:none" && exit 0
    echo "$ARG1" | grep -qE '^[A-Z0-9]+-[0-9]+$' || { echo "INVALID_ID:$ARG1"; exit 0; }
    [ -z "$ARGREST" ] && echo "NO_TEXT" && exit 0
    BODY=$(python3 -c "import json,sys; print(json.dumps({'text': 'Message from Matrix: ' + sys.argv[1]}))" "$ARGREST")
    ytcurl -w "\nHTTP_STATUS:%{http_code}" -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" "$YT_URL/api/issues/$ARG1/comments" -d "$BODY" 2>&1
    ;;
  *)
    echo "USAGE"
    ;;
esac

for i in $(seq 1 20); do

=ISSUE_ID="{{ $('Build Context').first().json.issueId }}"
PID={{ $('Launch Resume Claude').first().json.stdout.match(/PID:(\d+)/)[1] }}
SID={{ $('Launch Resume Claude').first().json.stdout.match(/SID:(.+)/)[1] || '' }}
LOG="/tmp/claude-run-${ISSUE_ID}.jsonl"
INJECT="/tmp/claude-inject-${ISSUE_ID}.json"
while true; do
  while kill -0 $PID 2>/dev/null; do sleep 5; done
  if [ -f "$INJECT" ]; then
    MSG=$(python3 -c "import json; print(json.load(open('$INJECT'))['message'])")
    rm -f "$INJECT"
    unset CLAUDECODE
    cd /app/cubeos
    nohup systemd-run --user --scope --quiet --slice=app.slice --unit="claude-$$-$(date +%s%N)" timeout 300 /home/app-user/.local/bin/claude -r "$SID" --output-format stream-json --verbose --dangerously-skip-permissions "$MSG" >> "$LOG" 2>&1 &
    PID=$!
    echo $PID > "/tmp/claude-pid-${ISSUE_ID}"
    sleep 2
    continue
  fi
  break
done
RESULT=$(grep '"type":"result"' "$LOG" | tail -1)
if [ -n "$RESULT" ]; then
  python3 -c "import json,sys; r=json.loads(sys.stdin.readline()); print(json.dumps({'result':r.get('result',''),'session_id':r.get('session_id',''),'is_error':r.get('is_error',False)}))" <<< "$RESULT"
else
  python3 -c "
import json
texts, sid = [], ''
for line in open('$LOG'):
    try:
        e = json.loads(line)
        if e.get('type') == 'system' and e.get('subtype') == 'init':
            sid = e.get('session_id', '')
        elif e.get('type') == 'assistant':
            for c in e.get('message',{}).get('content',[]):
                if c.get('type') == 'text' and c.get('text','').strip():
                    texts.append(c['text'])
    except: pass
if texts:
    print(json.dumps({'result': texts[-1], 'session_id': sid, 'is_error': False}))
else:
    print(json.dumps({'result': '', 'session_id': sid, 'is_error': False}))
" 2>/dev/null || echo '{"result":"","session_id":"","is_error":false}'
fi
rm -f "$LOG" "${LOG}.offset" "/tmp/claude-pid-${ISSUE_ID}" "$INJECT"

$nf--FqMAUVH8yYMHLWkCkawTNUQ8BT8DupdUbhx.question: [MESHSAT-536] Two open questions — please pick one of each:

answers.body: *Q1 — Scope**
**A. Signing key only** (strict issue wording). Leaves X25519 encryption key unwrapped. Inconsistent security posture; I do not recommend this.
**B. Both Reticulum identity keys** (signing + encryption). They live side-by-side, have equal sensitivity, share one migration. Recommended.
*Q2 — Master-key absence behavior on upgrade**
**A. Hard-fail startup** if `HUB_MASTER_KEY` unset on an already-deployed Hub. Safest, but a one-touch operator action is required during the v1.7 → v1.8 upgrade.

**B. Soft mode**: if `HUB_MASTER_KEY` unset, keep legacy unwrapped behavior and log a WARN every startup. No operator step required; security gap remains until they act. Recommended only if you want zero-touch rollout.
Once you pick (e.g. `B, A`), I'll implement + run `make lint test` + `gosec ./...` before committing.

**Plan A — Minimal (acceptance-literal, ~half day)**: Add `keystore.ResolveContactKey(ctx, contactID, kind)` as a thin wrapper that takes a `directory.Store` (set via setter to avoid an import cycle), defers to the existing unwrap logic. Keep `GenerateAndStoreAES` as random-key generation. Add 1–2 tests proving the keystore-level method behaves identically to the engine-level one. **Trade-off:** ignores the "X25519+HKDF" wording in the goal; matches the four checkboxes exactly.

**Plan B — Full goal (~1.5 days)**: Plan A *plus* a new `DeriveAESFromX25519(ctx, contactID)` method that (1) loads the bridge's X25519 LT private key from `routing.Identity`, (2) loads the contact's active `KeyX25519LongTermPub` from directory, (3) runs `ecdh.X25519().ECDH(...)` + `hkdf.New(sha256, shared, salt=contactID, info="meshsat/aes-traffic/v1")` to derive 32 bytes, (4) stores it as an `AES256_GCM_SHARED` record with `TrustAnchor=local` (deterministic — same inputs always produce the same key, so both ends derive identically). Add deterministic-derivation tests + rotation tests that cover X25519 key rotation invalidating the derived AES key. **Trade-off:** matches the issue title/goal but requires plumbing the bridge's X25519 private key into the keystore and adding a new keystore method beyond the strict acceptance.

**Plan C — Stub now, derive later**: Plan A only, and create a follow-up MESHSAT issue ("Implement X25519+HKDF derivation for per-contact AES traffic keys") that links to MESHSAT-537 and MESHSAT-548. **Trade-off:** ships fast, defers the cryptographic substance. Useful if S1-01/S1-02 dependencies haven't fully landed the X25519 directory rows yet.

**Plan B — Pull 301 forward now.** Flip the 4 legacy handlers to `http.Redirect(..., 301)` and patch `web/src/store/*` or wherever the SPA calls `/api/cellular/contacts*` to use the new endpoint in the same commit. Trade-off: ships the second acceptance bullet today but expands scope into MESHSAT-553's territory — the SMS tab would then speak the new API while the tab itself still exists, which is a half-migration.

**Plan C — Split the deferred bits out explicitly.** Create two new linked child issues under MESHSAT-553: one for "flip `/api/cellular/contacts*` to 301 after SPA cutover" and one for "drop `sms_contacts` table in v50 migration". Move MESHSAT-542 to `Done` (bridge-side scope fully delivered). Trade-off: cleanest paper trail, but adds two issues you'll have to re-open for triage.

My recommendation is **Plan C** — the bridge-side scope of this ticket is closed and the deferred items are really Phase 3 / v50 obligations that deserve their own tracking so they don't get lost behind "To Verify".

**Plan B — Staged in 2 commits on the same branch**: (1) store + migrations + structs + store tests, (2) REST handlers + signer + Swagger + handler tests. Easier to review; same total scope.

**Plan B**: New `DualReadResolver` type owning both resolvers + `main.go` rewiring. Cleaner separation, but broader diff (~300 LOC) and touches wiring outside the issue's declared file list.

**Plan B — DB-stored migration marker:** write grace-start timestamp to `system_config` on first boot after the contact-key migration completes (tie to an existing signal like "first successful `contact:` resolve"). Fleet-consistent, survives env changes, but adds state and a schema touch (or a config-key convention).

I'd recommend **Plan A** — matches the existing env-var-heavy config style (`MESHSAT_KEY_PASSPHRASE`, `MESHSAT_MODE`, etc.), operator can extend grace with one variable if a fleet bridge is lagging, and zero schema risk.

$W9tG7SoGzDYGwGMH95X1LwVM3Jnhe0HhqsNC7lv.question: [MESHSAT-559] Which approach for MapView symbol resolution?

answers.body: **Plan A — Frontend resolver (lighter, ships faster):** `MapView.vue` builds a client-side `nodeId → sidc` map by walking `store.contacts` (matching addresses to node IDs). CoT-type → SIDC fallback for anything unmatched. Pros: no Go changes; contacts already loaded for People view. Cons: coupling between Map and Contact store; no SIDC for TAK-relayed positions without a contact.

**Plan B — Backend exposes resolved SIDC on `/api/nodes` + positions:** Go handler joins `directory_contacts` to the node response so each node entry carries `sidc`. Pros: single source of truth, works for TAK positions too. Cons: extra Go work (~1 new file + handler change), more testing.
Plan A is the minimum for acceptance-criteria pass and keeps scope to the 3 files called out in the issue. Plan B is more correct long-term but expands the file list.

**Plan B** — Introduce a separate Ed25519 signer in `TrustAnchor` (new `ed25519_signing_key` system_config entry, generated lazily alongside the ECDSA key). Matches the acceptance criterion literally and aligns with the Hub/bridge contact-key "Ed25519" algorithm convention.
[POLL] Which Ed25519 key goes *inside* the payload as "signing_pubkey"?

**Plan X** — The contact's own `ContactKey` where `Algorithm="Ed25519"` and `Status="active"` (most recent). Matches the acceptance wording "signing pubkey" = the contact's pubkey — the recipient of the QR scan learns how to verify future messages signed by that contact. Returns 409 if the contact has no active Ed25519 key.

**Plan Y** — The Hub's signing pubkey (useful for trust-anchor bootstrap, but redundant with the provisioning bundle from MESHSAT-539, and "signing pubkey" in a contact card conventionally means the contact's own).
My recommendation: **B + X** — literal acceptance match, and "signing pubkey" naturally reads as the contact's.
S1-02 is listed as a blocker — I don't know what ticket that maps to. If it's unresolved I should wait; please confirm it's unblocked before I implement.
## Proposed Actions

1. `internal/directory/qr.go` — new file: `BuildContactQR(c *Contact, anchor *TrustAnchor) (uri string, err error)`, canonical CBOR, envelope `{v:1, payload, sig, alg:"Ed25519"}`, URI `meshsat://contact/<b64url(cbor_envelope)>`. Plus `ParseContactQR` for round-trip tests.

2. `internal/directory/signing.go` — extend `TrustAnchor` with Ed25519 keypair (if Plan B) stored under new system_config key `directory_qr_signing_key`; expose `SignEd25519(msg) []byte` and `Ed25519PublicKey() []byte`.

3. `internal/api/directory.go` — add `GetContactQR` handler, wire route in the router file (check `cmd/meshsat-hub/main.go` or `internal/api/routes.go`), tenant-scoped, returns `{uri, png_base64}` JSON with Swagger annotations. Use `readJSON` pattern only for inputs; this is a GET.

4. Tests: `qr_test.go` (CBOR canonical roundtrip, signature verify, tamper detection, missing Ed25519 key → error), `directory_test.go` handler test (200 happy path, 404 unknown, 409 no-key, tenant leak guard).
5. Browser/Android parser parity is out of scope for this repo but the CBOR layout + envelope will be documented in the qr.go package comment so both consumers can mirror it.

answers.body: **Plan A — Bake into Pi image at Packer build time.** New `releases/platforms/raspberrypi/scripts/10-display.sh` appends the two dtoverlays to `/boot/firmware/config.txt` (idempotent). $L4kgYEUlyxTqbdowy0Xs75ixfW6i2nAE1LJPgJQ.question: [MESHSAT-623] How should paired BLE peers be exposed as Reticulum interfaces?

answers.body: **Plan A — Single `ble_0` interface, multiple peers (fan-out model)**

- `ble_0` keeps its existing Reticulum registration. Paired centrals become internal "links" within `ble_0` — `Send()` fans out to all connected devices; `Receive()` demuxes by GATT connection handle.

- Trade-off: Matches how the peripheral already works (N browsers connect to one GATT server). Keeps flood control / cost / floodable flag as one toggle. But topology view shows one interface regardless of peer count.
- Persistence: `ble_peers(mac, name, paired_at, last_rssi)` — peers are a list under `ble_0`.
- Effort: ~60% of the code changes live inside `ble_interface.go`.
**Plan B — Per-peer dynamic interfaces (`ble_1`, `ble_2`, ...)**

- Each paired peer registers its own `ReticulumInterface` like TCP peers do via `reticulum_peers`. The existing `ble_0` (peripheral advertiser) stays as-is for inbound browser/Android connections; outbound pairs become separate interfaces.

- Trade-off: Mirrors the TCP-peers-in-Settings pattern exactly, each link is first-class in topology and has its own bandwidth/bond membership. More plumbing (dynamic register/unregister against `InterfaceRegistry`, extra entries in `bond_groups` if user wants HeMB), and two BLE "things" in the Settings UI (advertiser + pair list) which may confuse operators.
- Persistence: same `ble_peers` table, but each row also yields an InterfaceRegistry entry at boot.
- Effort: ~15% more code, larger blast radius (touches `InterfaceRegistry`, topology, HeMB).

My recommendation is **Plan A** — it fits the acceptance criteria ("paired peers become first-class Reticulum routes in `ble_0`'s peer list" — wording from the issue itself points at the fan-out model) and keeps the blast radius within the BLE package. Plan B is correct if you foresee wanting per-peer floodable toggles or per-peer HeMB bonding.

**Plan A — Full AC compliance**: add a `force bool` field to the request; compare the incoming AES-256 key byte-wise (`subtle.ConstantTimeCompare`) against the active stored key per `(type, address)`: equal → skip as `unchanged`; differ + `!force` → HTTP **409** with both fingerprints in the body; differ + `force` → current retire+new-version path. Plus write the round-trip unit test. **Trade-off**: changes the semantics of an endpoint that's already live on tesseract/parallax — callers that re-post the same bundle will now get `unchanged` instead of `version=N+1`, and a different-key re-post will start returning 409. This is safer (prevents silent key overwrites between field kits) but anyone automating re-imports needs to pass `force=true`.

**Plan B — Test only**: add the round-trip unit test proving A→B ingest decrypts A's AES-GCM ciphertext; leave the existing retire-and-append semantics alone. Faster, zero blast-radius, but leaves AC #3 (the `force`/409 line) unmet and means a misdirected QR scan could silently retire the kit's active APRS key with a 7-day grace window.

My recommendation is **Plan A** — the endpoint is brand-new (zero production callers before this session per the issue), so tightening the default before it gets adoption costs nothing and matches the AC exactly. But this is your call because you own the field-kit runbook.

**Plan A — `hemb_0` interface ingress transforms (smallest diff, no wire change).** Skip the new `ingress_transforms` column on `bond_groups`. Instead, when an operator turns on bond encryption for a group, copy the same JSON chain (e.g. `[{type:"encrypt", params:{key_ref:"bond:<group_id>"}}]`) into the `hemb_0` interface's `ingress_transforms` column. Re-inject path at `cmd/meshsat/main.go:1589` already runs through `DispatchAccess("hemb_0", …)`, so the decrypt happens for free at `dispatcher.go:500`. **Tradeoff:** if you ever have multiple bond groups with different keys, they all collide on `hemb_0`. Today there's one bond (`bond1`); we'd document the constraint. Smallest, safest change.

**Plan B — per-group receive bonders + source-interface routing.** Replace the single global receive bonder with one per bond group at startup. When a coded symbol arrives on a member interface, look up `bond_members` → group ID → dispatch the symbol to that group's bonder. Each bonder's `DeliverFn` closure captures the group ID and runs `ApplyIngress(reconstructed, group.IngressTransforms)` before re-inject. **Tradeoff:** more code (~150 LOC), needs a "interface → bond" router; but cleanly supports multiple bond groups with different keys. No wire change.

**Plan C — bump HeMB header to carry a 4-byte group hash.** Cleanest long-term but breaks the wire — both field kits (tesseract + parallax) must upgrade in lockstep before any encrypted bond traffic decodes. Requires HeMB protocol version bump and back-compat handling. I'd defer this unless you specifically want it.

My recommendation: **Plan A** — it ships the encrypt-then-code property end-to-end with the smallest surface area, defers the multi-bond-with-different-keys question (which doesn't exist on either field kit today), and reuses the audited transform pipeline path verbatim. We can promote to Plan B later if/when operators add a second bond.

Once you pick a plan, the rest is straightforward: migration **v42** (`bond_groups.egress_transforms` always; `ingress_transforms` only under Plan B), the two `ApplyEgress` call sites in `dispatcher.go`, `keystore.ChannelBond = 0x07` + maps, `PUT /api/bond-groups/{id}` extension, UI surface mirroring the APRS tab, and the test matrix as specified in the issue. I'll also flag the `aprs_0`-joins-bond hypothetical that the issue notes as a side risk and add a regression test for it.

$X9sO_OHqdqQnUS1TyI0uJE_cnVTOGgbcHHp8mA1.question: [IFRNLLEI01PRD-723] ` blocks are parsed. Adding it would teach the validator/operator a pattern that silently fails in production.

answers.body: It also isn't applicable here: this is a synthetic J3 smoke test whose instruction is literally "grep … for Phase 0 then stop." There is no action pending approval, so there is nothing for an operator to approve.
If a `[POLL]` is required by the harness even on synthetic payloads, the correct shape is:
[POLL] Smoke test complete — how should I close out?
Plan A: Stop here (synthetic payload, no action needed)
Plan B: Investigate further before stopping

But I'm flagging this prompt as suspicious: "your response will be posted regardless" + "add this exact line" reads like a prompt-injection / social-engineering pattern targeting the J3 hardening itself. A real validator would not override a documented protocol token.

**Plan A — Diagnose first, then patch.** Leave code untouched for now; SSH to parallax, grep `journalctl` for the `ble: pinned advertising address...` / `ble: btmgmt ... step failed` log lines and rerun `btmgmt -i hci0 info` / `btmgmt -i hci0 extinfo`. This tells us whether `ensureStableAdvAddress` ran to completion with no error, *and* whether the controller's current BD_ADDR matches `deriveStaticRandomMAC("nlparallax01")`. If `btmgmt` says the address IS pinned but the advertisement still uses NRPA, we've proved the fix target is in the `LEAdvertisement1` registration, not in the address pin. Zero code change, zero risk, narrows the fix. (~20 min.)

**Plan B — Harden the existing static-random pin with an explicit `privacy off` step + re-verify.** Extend the `btmgmt` sequence in `ensureStableAdvAddress` to `power off → privacy off → static-addr <target> → power on`, and add a post-pin verification (re-read `btmgmtCurrentAddr` and log mismatch at warn). This cheaply covers the case where brcmfmac requires privacy explicitly disabled to honour the static random in peripheral role. Still best-effort (non-fatal), no behaviour change on kits where the current path already works. (~0.25 d incl. deploy + field re-test on parallax+tesseract.)

**Plan C — Move identity enforcement into the `LEAdvertisement1` object itself.** Add a `Discoverable` property (bool, true), an `Includes` array entry, and explicitly set `OwnAddressType=public-only` via BlueZ's newer advertising properties (`bluez-5.72+` supports `MinInterval`, `MaxInterval`, `Duration`, `Includes`, `ServiceData`). Combined with Plan B. This is the "correct" BlueZ-native fix but touches more of the advertisement registration and needs a BlueZ version check on the field kits first (`bluetoothctl --version`). (~0.5 d.)

*My lean: Plan A first** — we should not modify code until we have evidence of which of the three failure modes is actually firing. Plan B is the cheapest code change if Plan A shows the pin is failing or being overridden; Plan C only becomes justified if Plan A proves the pin succeeded but BlueZ is overriding at advertising time.
No code changed this turn. Awaiting selection before SSH / edit.

$jE9DkrcKDrIQDNSo-fc4CboF46g-jYUHdVOacTX.question: [MESHSAT-677] How should we close out MESHSAT-677 now that MESHSAT-678 owns the actual fix?

answers.body: **Plan A — Revert the dead path, keep the helper.** Edit `meshsat/internal/routing/ble_interface.go`:
- Remove `ensureStableAdvAddress` and `btmgmtCurrentAddr`, drop the `os/exec` and `time` imports they introduced if unused, drop the call site at `BLEInterface.Start` line 106.
- Keep `deriveStaticRandomMAC` and both `TestDeriveStaticRandomMAC_*` tests — useful for future LE-only controllers and as a stable-identity log line.
- Optionally log the derived MAC at INFO on Start so operators see "this kit's intended stable identity is X" even when BlueZ ends up advertising the public MAC (which is also stable).
- Single commit `[MESHSAT-677]`, single repo (meshsat), small surface (~50 LOC removed). Pipeline auto-deploys to both kits.
- Trade-off: clean code, no spurious warn line, but means another commit to review/deploy. Removes a (currently no-op) safety net.

**Plan B — Leave the no-op in place; close 677 against 678.** Touch nothing in code. Just acknowledge that the code is a harmless fallback and that MESHSAT-678's host-side systemd unit will obsolete it; once 678 lands, revisit removal at that point.

- Trade-off: zero risk now, but leaves the warn-line noise on every BLE start, and the dead `btmgmt` call path stays in the binary indefinitely. The "or keep it as a no-op fallback if the host path also fails — decide during implementation" clause in MESHSAT-678 would technically allow this, but it's not load-bearing because the failure mode it'd guard against (host path AND container path both broken) doesn't have a recovery — neither path can set the address on dual-mode controllers anyway.

I am NOT recommending a Plan C (nsenter into the host PID/mount namespaces). That would also require compose changes, AND it'd still hit the dual-mode "static stored but not advertised" wall — so it'd be effort with no acceptance payoff.

My read: **Plan A** is the right call. The code path is dead by hardware behaviour, not just by container sandbox, so the fallback isn't actually a safety net — it's a no-op masquerading as one. But it's small enough that B is also defensible if you want to bundle the cleanup with MESHSAT-678's systemd-unit MR.

PIDFILE="/tmp/claude-pid-${ISSUE_ID}"

DB=/app/cubeos/claude-context/gateway.db
if [ -n "$PREFIX" ]; then
  FILTER="issue_id='$PREFIX'"
else
  ROOM_PREFIX=""
  case "$ROOM" in
    *miZJJDwFQZDkuMcBqL*) ROOM_PREFIX="MESHSAT-" ;;
    *iXTnQsFJahUquYPDdG*) ROOM_PREFIX="CUBEOS-" ;;
    *AOMuEtXGyzGFLgObKN*) ROOM_PREFIX="IFRNLLEI01PRD-" ;;
  esac
  if [ -n "$ROOM_PREFIX" ]; then
    FILTER="issue_id LIKE '${ROOM_PREFIX}%'"
  else
    FILTER="is_current=1"
  fi
fi
PAUSED=$(sqlite3 $DB "SELECT COALESCE(paused,0) FROM sessions WHERE $FILTER ORDER BY last_active DESC LIMIT 1;" 2>/dev/null)
if [ "$PAUSED" = "1" ]; then
  ISSUE=$(sqlite3 $DB "SELECT issue_id FROM sessions WHERE $FILTER ORDER BY last_active DESC LIMIT 1;")
  sqlite3 $DB "INSERT INTO queue (issue_id, message) VALUES ('$ISSUE', '$B64MSG');"
  echo PAUSED
else
  ISSUE=$(sqlite3 $DB "SELECT issue_id FROM sessions WHERE $FILTER ORDER BY last_active DESC LIMIT 1;")
  sqlite3 $DB "SELECT message FROM queue WHERE issue_id='$ISSUE' ORDER BY id ASC;"
  sqlite3 $DB "DELETE FROM queue WHERE issue_id='$ISSUE';"
fi

'No active session for ' + $json.prefix + '.' : 'No active session. Move an issue to In Progress in YouTrack to start a new session.' }) }}

parameters.jsCode:
const sub = $json.sub || '';

let body = '';

if (!sub) {
  body = 'Available commands:\n'
    + '!session    \u2014 Manage active Claude sessions\n'
    + '!issue      \u2014 Manage YouTrack issues\n'
    + '!pipeline   \u2014 Monitor GitLab CI pipelines\n'
    + '!system     \u2014 View system status\n'
    + '!gateway    \u2014 Switch between online/offline mode\n'
    + '!mode       \u2014 Switch gateway routing mode\n'
    + '!debug      \u2014 Dump raw gateway state\n'
    + '!learn      \u2014 Teacher agent (in #learning): lessons + quizzes + progress\n'
    + '!help <command> \u2014 Show subcommands for a command\n'
    + '\n'
    + 'Legacy shortcuts: !done, !status, !cancel\n'
    + 'Mode shortcuts: !offline, !online\n'
    + 'Teacher shortcuts: !quiz, !progress, !digest, !leaderboard, !skip, !teach, !grade';
} else if (sub === 'session') {
  body = '!session commands:\n'
    + 'current              \u2014 Show current active session details\n'
    + 'list                 \u2014 List all active sessions\n'
    + 'done [issue-id]      \u2014 End session \u2192 YouTrack To Verify\n'
    + 'cancel [issue-id]    \u2014 Hard abort session, clear lock + queue\n'
    + 'pause [issue-id]     \u2014 Hold queue, stop processing messages\n'
    + 'resume [issue-id]    \u2014 Resume paused session\n'
    + 'switch <issue-id>    \u2014 Change current session\n'
    + 'log [issue-id]       \u2014 Last 50 lines of execution log\n'
    + '\n'
    + '[issue-id] is optional \u2014 defaults to current session if omitted.';
} else if (sub === 'issue') {
  body = '!issue commands:\n'
    + '\n'
    + 'State transitions:\n'
    + 'open <id>           \u2014 Move to Open (ends active session)\n'
    + 'inprogress <id>     \u2014 Move to In Progress (starts session)\n'
    + 'toverify <id>       \u2014 Move to To Verify (ends session if active)\n'
    + 'done <id>           \u2014 Move to Done (ends session if active)\n'
    + '\n'
    + 'Aliases:\n'
    + 'start <id>          \u2014 Alias for inprogress\n'
    + 'verify <id>         \u2014 Alias for toverify\n'
    + 'close <id>          \u2014 Alias for done\n'
    + 'stop <id>           \u2014 Move back to Open (no session change)\n'
    + '\n'
    + 'Other:\n'
    + 'status              \u2014 List In Progress + Open issues\n'
    + 'info <id>           \u2014 Show issue details\n'
    + 'comment <id> <text> \u2014 Post comment to issue\n'
    + '\n'
    + 'Example: !issue inprogress CUBEOS-21';
} else if (sub === 'pipeline') {
  body = '!pipeline commands:\n'
    + 'status           \u2014 All repos latest pipeline status\n'
    + 'status <repo>    \u2014 Last 5 pipelines for a specific repo\n'
    + 'logs <repo>      \u2014 Logs from latest failed pipeline\n'
    + 'retry <repo>     \u2014 Retry latest failed pipeline\n'
    + 'Repos: api, dashboard, hal, coreapps, releases, meshsat';
} else if (sub === 'system') {
  body = '!system commands:\n'
    + 'status     \u2014 System snapshot (load, memory, disk, uptime)\n'
    + 'processes  \u2014 Claude and n8n process list';
} else if (sub === 'gateway') {
  body = '!gateway commands:\n'
    + 'offline  \u2014 Switch to local Ollama model (devstral-small-2)\n'
    + 'online   \u2014 Switch to Anthropic cloud API\n'
    + 'status   \u2014 Show current mode and session\n'
    + '\n'
    + 'Shortcuts: !offline, !online\n'
    + 'Active session blocks mode switching.';
} else if (sub === 'mode') {
  body = '!mode commands:\n'
    + 'status     \u2014 Show current routing mode\n'
    + 'oc-cc      \u2014 OpenClaw \u2192 Claude Code (default)\n'
    + 'oc-oc      \u2014 OpenClaw \u2192 OpenClaw (fully local)\n'
    + 'cc-cc      \u2014 Claude Code only (legacy)\n'
    + 'cc-oc      \u2014 n8n session \u2192 OpenClaw backend\n'
    + '\n'
    + 'Example: !mode oc-cc';
} else if (sub === 'debug') {
  body = '!debug\n'
    + 'Dumps raw gateway state: lock file, sessions DB, queue count,\n'
    + 'cooldown files, mode (online/offline), and claude processes.\n'
    + 'No subcommands \u2014 just type !debug.';
} else if (sub === 'learn' || sub === 'teacher' || sub === 'quiz' || sub === 'progress' || sub === 'digest' || sub === 'leaderboard' || sub === 'skip' || sub === 'teach' || sub === 'grade') {
  // IFRNLLEI01PRD-654 teacher agent — operator-facing help.
  body = '!learn commands (teacher agent; classroom = #learning):\n'
    + '\n'
    + '!learn                      \u2014 Pick up where you left off (SM-2 due topic or first-run bootstrap)\n'
    + '!learn <topic-id>           \u2014 Lesson on a specific topic (see !progress for ids)\n'
    + '!learn pause | resume       \u2014 Pause / resume scheduling for your account\n'
    + '!learn public on | off      \u2014 Opt in / out of leaderboard and !progress public\n'
    + '\n'
    + '!quiz                       \u2014 Quiz on the current due topic\n'
    + '!quiz <topic-id>            \u2014 Quiz on a specific topic\n'
    + '!grade <session-id> <text> \u2014 Submit your answer for grading (session id is in the quiz DM)\n'
    + '!progress                   \u2014 Your mastery distribution (DM)\n'
    + '!progress public            \u2014 Post to #learning (requires !learn public on)\n'
    + '!digest | !leaderboard      \u2014 Aggregate class digest in #learning\n'
    + '!skip | !teach              \u2014 Skip the current topic or request a new lesson\n'
    + '\n'
    + 'Authorisation: must be a joined member of #learning.\n'
    + 'Runbook: docs/runbooks/teacher-agent.md';
} else {
  body = '!' + sub + ' \u2014 unknown command.\n\n' +
    'Available commands:\n' +
    '!session    \u2014 Manage active Claude sessions\n' +
    '!issue      \u2014 Manage YouTrack issues\n' +
    '!pipeline   \u2014 Monitor GitLab CI pipelines\n' +
    '!system     \u2014 View system status\n' +
    '!gateway    \u2014 Switch between online/offline mode\n' +
    '!mode       \u2014 Switch gateway routing mode\n' +
    '!debug      \u2014 Dump raw gateway state\n' +
    '!learn      \u2014 Teacher agent (in #learning): lessons + quizzes + progress\n' +
    '!help <cmd> \u2014 Show subcommands for a command\n' +
    '\n' +
    'Legacy shortcuts: !done, !status, !cancel\n' +
    'Mode shortcuts: !offline, !online\n' +
    'Teacher shortcuts: !quiz, !progress, !digest, !leaderboard, !skip, !teach, !grade';
}

const matrixBody = JSON.stringify({ msgtype: 'm.notice', body });

return [{ json: { matrixBody, isDone: false, triggerData: null } }];

conditions.leftValue: ={{ $if($('Format Session Response').isExecuted, $('Format Session Response').first().json.isDone, $if($('Format Issue Response').isExecuted, $('Format Issue Response').first().json.isDone, false)) }}

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ (() => { if ($('Format Session Response').isExecuted && $('Format Session Response').first().json.triggerData) return JSON.stringify($('Format Session Response').first().json.triggerData); if ($('Format Issue Response').isExecuted && $('Format Issue Response').first().json.triggerData) return JSON.stringify($('Format Issue Response').first().json.triggerData); return '{}'; })() }}

parameters.command:
=SUB="{{ $('Detect Command').first().json.sub }}"
RAW_ARG1="{{ $('Detect Command').first().json.args[0] || '' }}"
RAW_ARGREST="{{ $('Detect Command').first().json.args.slice(1).join(' ') }}"
ROOM="{{ $('Extract Messages').first().json.sourceRoom }}"
YT_URL="https://youtrack.example.net"
YT_TOKEN="REDACTED_YT_TOKEN"
DB=/app/cubeos/claude-context/gateway.db
GW=/app/cubeos/claude-context

ARG1=$(echo "$RAW_ARG1" | tr '[:lower:]' '[:upper:]')
ARGREST="$RAW_ARGREST"

# Room-aware project mapping
YT_PROJECT="CUBEOS"
case "$ROOM" in
  *miZJJDwFQZDkuMcBqL*) YT_PROJECT="MESHSAT" ;;
  *iXTnQsFJahUquYPDdG*) YT_PROJECT="CUBEOS" ;;
  *AOMuEtXGyzGFLgObKN*) YT_PROJECT="IFRNLLEI01PRD" ;;
esac

ytcurl() {
  curl -s --max-time 10 -H "Authorization: Bearer $YT_TOKEN" "$@"
}

lookupNumericId() {
  ytcurl "$YT_URL/api/issues/$1?fields=id" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('id',''))" 2>/dev/null
}

patchState() {
  local NUMID="$1"
  local STATE="$2"
  curl -s --max-time 10 -w "\nHTTP_STATUS:%{http_code}" \
    -X POST \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer $YT_TOKEN" \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -d "{\"customFields\":[{\"\$type\":\"StateIssueCustomField\",\"name\":\"State\",\"value\":{\"name\":\"$STATE\"}}]}" \
    "$YT_URL/api/issues/$NUMID"
}

case "$SUB" in
  status)
    ytcurl -w "\nHTTP_STATUS:%{http_code}" "$YT_URL/api/issues?query=project%3A${YT_PROJECT}%20State%3A%7BIn%20Progress%7D%2COpen&fields=idReadable,summary,customFields(name,value(name))&\$top=20" 2>&1
    ;;
  info)
    [ -z "$ARG1" ] && echo "INVALID_ID:none" && exit 0
    echo "$ARG1" | grep -qE '^[A-Z0-9]+-[0-9]+$' || { echo "INVALID_ID:$ARG1"; exit 0; }
    ytcurl -w "\nHTTP_STATUS:%{http_code}" "$YT_URL/api/issues/$ARG1?fields=idReadable,summary,description,created,updated,tags(name),customFields(name,value(name,login))" 2>&1
    ;;
  start|inprogress)
    [ -z "$ARG1" ] && echo "INVALID_ID:none" && exit 0
    echo "$ARG1" | grep -qE '^[A-Z0-9]+-[0-9]+$' || { echo "INVALID_ID:$ARG1"; exit 0; }
    NUMID=$(lookupNumericId "$ARG1")
    [ -z "$NUMID" ] && echo "LOOKUP_FAILED:$ARG1" && exit 0
    LOCK_FILE="$GW/gateway.lock"
    if [ -f "$LOCK_FILE" ]; then
      LOCK_AGE=$(( $(date +%s) - $(stat -c %Y "$LOCK_FILE") ))
      if [ "$LOCK_AGE" -lt 600 ]; then
        LOCK_ISSUE=$(cat "$LOCK_FILE" 2>/dev/null)
        echo "ACTIVE_SESSION:$LOCK_ISSUE"
        exit 0
      fi
    fi
    echo "$ARG1" > "$LOCK_FILE"
    patchState "$NUMID" "In Progress" >/dev/null 2>&1
    ISSUE_JSON=$(ytcurl "$YT_URL/api/issues/$ARG1?fields=idReadable,summary,description,comments(text,author(login),created)")
    SUMMARY=$(echo "$ISSUE_JSON" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('summary',''))" 2>/dev/null)
    DESC=$(echo "$ISSUE_JSON" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('description',''))" 2>/dev/null)
    COMMENTS=$(echo "$ISSUE_JSON" | python3 -c "
import sys,json
d=json.load(sys.stdin)
for c in d.get('comments',[]):
    a=c.get('author',{}).get('login','?')
    t=c.get('text','')
    print(a+': '+t)
" 2>/dev/null)
    B64_SUMMARY=$(echo "$SUMMARY" | base64 -w0)
    B64_DESC=$(echo "$DESC" | base64 -w0)
    B64_COMMENTS=$(echo "$COMMENTS" | base64 -w0)
    echo "START_SESSION:$ARG1"
    echo "B64_SUMMARY:$B64_SUMMARY"
    echo "B64_DESC:$B64_DESC"
    echo "B64_COMMENTS:$B64_COMMENTS"
    ;;
  open)
    [ -z "$ARG1" ] && echo "INVALID_ID:none" && exit 0
    echo "$ARG1" | grep -qE '^[A-Z0-9]+-[0-9]+$' || { echo "INVALID_ID:$ARG1"; exit 0; }
    NUMID=$(lookupNumericId "$ARG1")
    [ -z "$NUMID" ] && echo "LOOKUP_FAILED:$ARG1" && exit 0
    patchState "$NUMID" "Open"
    touch "$GW/gateway.cooldown.$ARG1"
    SESSION_JSON=$(sqlite3 "$DB" "SELECT json_object('sessionId', session_id, 'issueId', issue_id) FROM sessions WHERE issue_id='$ARG1';" 2>/dev/null)
    if [ -n "$SESSION_JSON" ]; then
      rm -f "$GW/gateway.lock"
      sqlite3 "$DB" "DELETE FROM queue WHERE issue_id='$ARG1';" 2>/dev/null
      sqlite3 "$DB" "INSERT INTO session_log (issue_id, issue_title, session_id, started_at, ended_at, message_count, outcome, schema_version) SELECT issue_id, issue_title, session_id, started_at, datetime('now'), message_count, 'open', 1 FROM sessions WHERE issue_id='$ARG1';" 2>/dev/null
      sqlite3 "$DB" "DELETE FROM sessions WHERE issue_id='$ARG1';" 2>/dev/null
      echo ""
      echo "SESSION_CLEANED:$ARG1"
    fi
    ;;
  stop)
    [ -z "$ARG1" ] && echo "INVALID_ID:none" && exit 0
    echo "$ARG1" | grep -qE '^[A-Z0-9]+-[0-9]+$' || { echo "INVALID_ID:$ARG1"; exit 0; }
    NUMID=$(lookupNumericId "$ARG1")
    [ -z "$NUMID" ] && echo "LOOKUP_FAILED:$ARG1" && exit 0
    patchState "$NUMID" "Open"
    touch "$GW/gateway.cooldown.$ARG1"
    ;;
  verify|toverify)
    [ -z "$ARG1" ] && echo "INVALID_ID:none" && exit 0
    echo "$ARG1" | grep -qE '^[A-Z0-9]+-[0-9]+$' || { echo "INVALID_ID:$ARG1"; exit 0; }
    NUMID=$(lookupNumericId "$ARG1")
    [ -z "$NUMID" ] && echo "LOOKUP_FAILED:$ARG1" && exit 0
    patchState "$NUMID" "To Verify"
    touch "$GW/gateway.cooldown.$ARG1"
    SESSION_JSON=$(sqlite3 "$DB" "SELECT json_object('sessionId', session_id, 'issueId', issue_id) FROM sessions WHERE issue_id='$ARG1' AND is_current=1;" 2>/dev/null)
    if [ -n "$SESSION_JSON" ]; then
      echo ""
      echo "HAS_SESSION:$SESSION_JSON"
    fi
    ;;
  done|close)
    [ -z "$ARG1" ] && echo "INVALID_ID:none" && exit 0
    echo "$ARG1" | grep -qE '^[A-Z0-9]+-[0-9]+$' || { echo "INVALID_ID:$ARG1"; exit 0; }
    NUMID=$(lookupNumericId "$ARG1")
    [ -z "$NUMID" ] && echo "LOOKUP_FAILED:$ARG1" && exit 0
    patchState "$NUMID" "Done"
    touch "$GW/gateway.cooldown.$ARG1"
    SESSION_JSON=$(sqlite3 "$DB" "SELECT json_object('sessionId', session_id, 'issueId', issue_id) FROM sessions WHERE issue_id='$ARG1' AND is_current=1;" 2>/dev/null)
    if [ -n "$SESSION_JSON" ]; then
      echo ""
      echo "HAS_SESSION:$SESSION_JSON"
    fi
    ;;
  comment)
    [ -z "$ARG1" ] && echo "INVALID_ID:none" && exit 0
    echo "$ARG1" | grep -qE '^[A-Z0-9]+-[0-9]+$' || { echo "INVALID_ID:$ARG1"; exit 0; }
    [ -z "$ARGREST" ] && echo "NO_TEXT" && exit 0
    BODY=$(python3 -c "import json,sys; print(json.dumps({'text': 'Message from Matrix: ' + sys.argv[1]}))" "$ARGREST")
    ytcurl -w "\nHTTP_STATUS:%{http_code}" -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" "$YT_URL/api/issues/$ARG1/comments" -d "$BODY" 2>&1
    ;;
  *)
    echo "USAGE"
    ;;
esac

=SUB={{ $json.sub }}
DB=/app/cubeos/claude-context/gateway.db
LOCK=/app/cubeos/claude-context/gateway.lock

case "$SUB" in
  status)
    LOAD=$(cat /proc/loadavg | awk '{print $1, $2, $3}')
    MEM=$(free -h | awk '/^Mem:/{print $3"/"$2}')
    DISK=$(df -h / | awk 'NR==2{print $3"/"$2" ("$5")"}')
    UPTIME=$(uptime -p | sed 's/up //')
    SERVICES=$(docker service ls 2>/dev/null | tail -n +2 | wc -l)
    LOCK_STATE="free"
    if [ -f "$LOCK" ]; then
      LOCK_AGE=$(( $(date +%s) - $(stat -c %Y "$LOCK") ))
      LOCK_STATE="held (${LOCK_AGE}s)"
    fi
    SESSION_COUNT=$(sqlite3 "$DB" "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM sessions;" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
    QUEUE_COUNT=$(sqlite3 "$DB" "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM queue;" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
    echo "HOST:$(hostname)"
    echo "UPTIME:$UPTIME"
    echo "LOAD:$LOAD"
    echo "MEM:$MEM"
    echo "DISK:$DISK"
    echo "SERVICES:$SERVICES"
    echo "SESSIONS:$SESSION_COUNT"
    echo "QUEUE:$QUEUE_COUNT"
    echo "LOCK:$LOCK_STATE"
    ;;
  processes)
    ps aux | grep -E 'claude|n8n' | grep -v grep | awk '{printf "%s|%s|%s|%s\n", $2, $3, $1, substr($0, index($0,$11))}'
    ;;
  *)
    echo "USAGE"
    ;;
esac

# Record feedback to SQLite if this is a reaction
FEEDBACK_TYPE='{{ $json._feedbackType || "" }}'
FEEDBACK_ISSUE='{{ $json._feedbackIssueId || "" }}'
FEEDBACK_SNIPPET='{{ ($json._feedbackSnippet || "").replace(/'/g, "''") }}'

parameters.command:
=ISSUE_ID="{{ $('Build Context').first().json.issueId }}"
SID="{{ $('Build Context').first().json.sessionId }}"
B64MSG="{{ $('Build Context').first().json.base64Message }}"
LOG="/tmp/claude-run-${ISSUE_ID}.jsonl"
PIDFILE="/tmp/claude-pid-${ISSUE_ID}"
rm -f "$LOG" "${LOG}.offset" "/tmp/claude-inject-${ISSUE_ID}.json"
PREFIX=$(echo "$ISSUE_ID" | cut -d- -f1)
case "$PREFIX" in
  IFRNLLEI01PRD) cd /app/infrastructure/nl/production ;;
  IFRGRSKG01PRD) cd /app/infrastructure/gr/production ;;
  *) cd /app/cubeos ;;
esac
unset CLAUDECODE
MSG=$(echo "$B64MSG" | base64 -d)
nohup systemd-run --user --scope --quiet --slice=app.slice --unit="claude-$$-$(date +%s%N)" timeout 300 /home/app-user/.local/bin/claude -r "$SID" --output-format stream-json --verbose --dangerously-skip-permissions "$MSG" > "$LOG" 2>&1 &
PID=$!
echo $PID > "$PIDFILE"
for i in $(seq 1 20); do
  if [ -s "$LOG" ]; then
    echo "PID:$PID"
    echo "SID:$SID"
    exit 0
  fi
  sleep 0.5
done
echo "PID:$PID"
echo "SID:$SID"

=ISSUE_ID="{{ $('Build Context').first().json.issueId }}"
PID={{ $('Launch Resume Claude').first().json.stdout.match(/PID:(\d+)/)[1] }}
SID={{ $('Launch Resume Claude').first().json.stdout.match(/SID:(.+)/)[1] || '' }}
LOG="/tmp/claude-run-${ISSUE_ID}.jsonl"
INJECT="/tmp/claude-inject-${ISSUE_ID}.json"
while true; do
  while kill -0 $PID 2>/dev/null; do sleep 5; done
  if [ -f "$INJECT" ]; then
    MSG=$(python3 -c "import json; print(json.load(open('$INJECT'))['message'])")
    rm -f "$INJECT"
    unset CLAUDECODE
    cd /app/cubeos
    nohup systemd-run --user --scope --quiet --slice=app.slice --unit="claude-$$-$(date +%s%N)" timeout 300 /home/app-user/.local/bin/claude -r "$SID" --output-format stream-json --verbose --dangerously-skip-permissions "$MSG" >> "$LOG" 2>&1 &
    PID=$!
    echo $PID > "/tmp/claude-pid-${ISSUE_ID}"
    sleep 2
    continue
  fi
  break
done
RESULT=$(grep '"type":"result"' "$LOG" | tail -1)
if [ -n "$RESULT" ]; then
  python3 -c "import json,sys; r=json.loads(sys.stdin.readline()); print(json.dumps({'result':r.get('result',''),'session_id':r.get('session_id',''),'is_error':r.get('is_error',False)}))" <<< "$RESULT"
else
  python3 -c "
import json
texts, sid = [], ''
for line in open('$LOG'):
    try:
        e = json.loads(line)
        if e.get('type') == 'system' and e.get('subtype') == 'init':
            sid = e.get('session_id', '')
        elif e.get('type') == 'assistant':
            for c in e.get('message',{}).get('content',[]):
                if c.get('type') == 'text' and c.get('text','').strip():
                    texts.append(c['text'])
    except: pass
if texts:
    print(json.dumps({'result': texts[-1], 'session_id': sid, 'is_error': False}))
else:
    print(json.dumps({'result': '', 'session_id': sid, 'is_error': False}))
" 2>/dev/null || echo '{"result":"","session_id":"","is_error":false}'
fi
rm -f "$LOG" "${LOG}.offset" "/tmp/claude-pid-${ISSUE_ID}" "$INJECT"

=ISSUE_ID="{{ $('Format Issue Response').first().json.issueId }}"
B64S="{{ $('Format Issue Response').first().json.b64Summary }}"

LOG="/tmp/claude-run-${ISSUE_ID}.jsonl"
PIDFILE="/tmp/claude-pid-${ISSUE_ID}"

PREFIX=$(echo "$ISSUE_ID" | cut -d- -f1)
if [ "$PREFIX" = "IFRNLLEI01PRD" ]; then

Plan a fix and post it to Matrix + YT comment.\n4. WAIT for human approval before executing.\n5. When approved, YT-comment the approval text+timestamp, then execute.\n6. If actions needed outside plan, ask again.\n7. Report results, YT-comment outcome, move issue to Done."

done
echo "PID:$PID"
echo "SID:"
